jokes.text: That ain't what's scaring me. You ain't got no drawers on!
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil wakes everyone up in a panic about Vivian having the baby
jokes.punchline: Will's more concerned about Phil being naked than the emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice - Will's street-smart perspective cutting through the chaos with inappropriate focus. Strong structure and economy.

jokes.text: Hilary, you circle the wagons.
jokes.setup: Will takes charge giving everyone emergency tasks
jokes.punchline: He gives Hilary a nonsensical Wild West command
jokes.explanation: Absurd Western reference in a birth emergency - classic Will randomness that became quotable.

jokes.text: Will, honey, what are you talking about? / I wish to God I knew, Aunt Viv.
jokes.setup: Vivian questions Will's bizarre 'circle the wagons' command
jokes.punchline: Will admits he has no idea what he's saying
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor at its finest - character acknowledging the absurdity of his own dialogue. Perfect economy and became highly quotable.

jokes.text: Carlton, honey, get up off the floor. My hand!
jokes.setup: Vivian has been squeezing someone's hand during contractions
jokes.punchline: Carlton is on the floor in pain from her grip strength
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup - pregnant woman's strong grip. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I find that hard to believe. / I think we all do, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton says he was once inside a woman's body (referring to birth)
jokes.punchline: Will and Ashley pile on with the obvious sexual interpretation
jokes.explanation: Low-hanging fruit sexual innuendo, but well-executed with Ashley piling on. Character-appropriate but not original.

jokes.text: I mean, getting woke up in the middle of the night... by a naked man running around the house.
jokes.setup: Will complains about being unable to do 'this' anymore
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's traumatized by seeing Uncle Phil naked
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reframes the opening trauma from Will's perspective. Solid misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Not you, G. Oh, never mind.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey thinks Will is referring to him as the naked man
jokes.punchline: Will's awkward clarification and quick topic change
jokes.explanation: Minor awkward moment that deflates previous joke. Functional but not memorable.

jokes.text: You think I don't? I feel like I ate a Buick.
jokes.setup: Phil wants to have the baby already
jokes.punchline: Vivian's creative metaphor for how huge she feels
jokes.explanation: Creative, relatable metaphor with good economy. Fresh take on pregnancy discomfort.

jokes.text: Look like a Winnebago from here.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she feels like she ate a Buick
jokes.punchline: Will ups the ante - she looks like an even bigger vehicle
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character voice - Will can't help but take it further. Good visual comedy.

jokes.text: Poor Mom? Poor me. I saw Uncle Phil naked.
jokes.setup: Hilary expresses sympathy for their pregnant mom
jokes.punchline: Will redirects sympathy to himself for the naked Phil trauma
jokes.explanation: Another callback but starting to wear thin. Still character-appropriate selfishness from Will.

jokes.text: And he was running.
jokes.setup: Will complains about seeing Uncle Phil naked
jokes.punchline: Added detail that makes the visual worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation technique - the added visual detail makes it worse. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Perhaps if we were both younger, and I didn't work for you.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she has a proposition for Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey misinterprets it as sexual, politely declines with British propriety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment - dignified misunderstanding with proper British deflection.

jokes.text: Not that kind of proposition, Geoffrey. I will give you $50 to drive me over a set of railroad tracks.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's sexual misinterpretation
jokes.punchline: Vivian's actual proposition is desperate and potentially dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff - from sexual to desperate pregnancy remedy. Shows Vivian's state of mind.

jokes.text: I will give you $50 to drive me over a set of railroad tracks.
jokes.setup: Vivian clarifying it's not a sexual proposition
jokes.punchline: Desperate plan to jostle the baby out via bumpy tracks
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd pregnancy desperation joke. Original concept that feels authentic to an overdue mother's mindset.

jokes.text: It's rather difficult to tell just by looking.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks who's having the baby, her or Phil
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's diplomatic way of saying Phil looks pregnant too
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey diplomatic insult. Implies Phil is fat without being direct - classic butler discretion.

jokes.text: And whatever you do, never ever feed her after midnight.
jokes.setup: Will giving warnings about interacting with pregnant Vivian
jokes.punchline: Gremlins movie reference comparing Vivian to a dangerous creature
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that works because everyone knows Gremlins rules. Comparing pregnant woman to gremlin is appropriately outrageous.

jokes.text: And whatever you do, never ever feed her after midnight.
jokes.setup: Will warns visitors about what not to mention to pregnant Vivian
jokes.punchline: References Gremlins movie rules
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference comparing pregnant Vivian to a Gremlin. Became highly quotable.

jokes.text: She snatched off the woman's wig? / No, Lester's.
jokes.setup: Story about Helen seeing Lester with another woman and snatching off 'the wig'
jokes.punchline: Clarification that Lester was wearing the wig, not the woman
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect setup/payoff structure. Subverts expectation about who wears the wig.

jokes.text: She snatched off the woman's wig? No, Lester's.
jokes.setup: Assumption that Helen snatched the other woman's wig
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Lester wears a wig
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. The surprise that the man wears a wig is both unexpected and embarrassing.

jokes.text: You've all met our butler, Chicken George?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey brings food and acts subservient
jokes.punchline: Will references Roots character, commenting on the racial dynamic
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural reference about race and class dynamics. Typical Will social commentary through humor.

jokes.text: If it get any bigger, it'll be in and out of Bel-Air at the same time.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Vivian's size and attitude
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated visual of her being so big she spans the whole neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Creative visual exaggeration tied to the show's setting. Good escalation technique.

jokes.text: All right, Hilary, you act like you're gonna hug her and then pin her arms down. / I know the chokehold. / Better.
jokes.setup: Planning to take Vivian's bell away
jokes.punchline: Carlton escalates from pinning to chokehold, Will approves
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation with Carlton surprisingly being more violent than expected. Good character subversion.

jokes.text: You hate me, don't you?
jokes.setup: Vivian calls for company after driving everyone away
jokes.punchline: No one responds, leading to her vulnerable question
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment - pregnant woman's insecurity after being terrible. The silence sells it.

jokes.text: What bell?
jokes.setup: Vivian asks Geoffrey if he's seen her bell
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey plays innocent about hiding it
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey deadpan delivery. Simple but effective innocent act.

jokes.text: What bell?
jokes.setup: Vivian asking about her missing bell
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey playing innocent after obviously hiding it
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan Geoffrey moment. Simple but effective with great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I know, and some electrolysis wouldn't hurt you, either.
jokes.setup: Ashley says she needed the salon treatment
jokes.punchline: Hilary suggests Ashley needs hair removal too
jokes.explanation: Typical Hilary shallow insult. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This could get ugly. We're family.
jokes.setup: Tension building between sisters at salon
jokes.punchline: Ashley's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that family fights are worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and truth - family fights are indeed uglier. Good Ashley wisdom moment.

jokes.text: Forget college, I'm going to beauty school.
jokes.setup: Carlton sees attractive women at the beauty salon
jokes.punchline: He immediately changes life plans for superficial reasons
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton impulsive decision-making. Character-appropriate but predictable horny teenager joke.

jokes.text: Look, if I'm not back in 20 minutes, be really happy for me.
jokes.setup: Carlton is going into the beauty salon
jokes.punchline: Implies he might get lucky with someone inside
jokes.explanation: Standard horny teenager joke but well-delivered in Carlton's optimistic style.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I think my water broke.
jokes.setup: Phil having sympathy pains throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Phil thinks his water broke like he's actually pregnant
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the sympathy pains running gag. Phil's complete delusion fits his character.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I think my water broke.
jokes.setup: Phil has been having sympathy pains
jokes.punchline: He thinks he's actually going into labor
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with Phil's sympathy pains taken to extreme. Character-appropriate neurosis.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, it's Vivian. She's having the baby. / We gotta get to the hospital! / Uncle Phil... we in the hospital.
jokes.setup: Phil panics about getting Vivian to the hospital
jokes.punchline: Will points out they're already there
jokes.explanation: Good panic/calm dynamic. Will's matter-of-fact correction of Phil's panic works well.

jokes.text: 402436, that's no phone number. / No, Uncle Phil, that's 40, 24, 36.
jokes.setup: Will gets paged with numbers Phil thinks is a phone number
jokes.punchline: Will clarifies it's a woman's measurements
jokes.explanation: Standard setup/punchline about Will's ladies. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: That's right, boy, this is a New Jack City.
jokes.setup: Carlton politely asks smoker to put out cigarette in elevator
jokes.punchline: Smoker references movie about crack dealers, intimidating Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that establishes the smoker as street tough vs. Carlton's preppy politeness.

jokes.text: You the big man that broke the elevator.
jokes.setup: Smoker was intimidating Carlton about being tough
jokes.punchline: Reveals Carlton actually broke the elevator, undermining his own toughness
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic reversal - the 'tough guy' smoker is trapped by nerdy Carlton's accident.

jokes.text: Now, we're on the same train of thought.
jokes.setup: Both Geoffrey and Vivian realize they need to beep Phil
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's understated response to obvious panic situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey understatement during crisis. His calm British manner contrasts with the emergency.

jokes.text: No, but you have really soft hands.
jokes.setup: Phil asks if Will is there in the dark elevator
jokes.punchline: Will reveals he's been holding the wrong person's hand
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and awkward moment. The hand-holding mixup creates uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: Ashley, you can't drive. / Grow up. I've been taking the car since I was 12. Come on.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey protests Ashley driving pregnant Vivian to hospital
jokes.punchline: Ashley casually admits to years of underage joyriding
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Ashley's matter-of-fact admission of criminal behavior delivered innocently.

jokes.text: Just kidding, a little elevator humor there.
jokes.setup: Worker jokes about going on break while people are trapped
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges his own bad joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about bad jokes. Worker character acknowledging his own weak humor works moderately well.

jokes.text: That's right, and you don't.
jokes.setup: Will noting the tough guy has a phone
jokes.punchline: Tough guy being petty about phone access
jokes.explanation: Shows the tough guy's petty nature. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Look, man, if you don't let me use your phone... I'm gonna start singing Paula Abdul's greatest hits.
jokes.setup: Will tries to intimidate smoker into letting him use phone
jokes.punchline: His threat is singing pop songs instead of violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of tough guy intimidation. Creative, unexpected threat that's somehow effective.

jokes.text: Look, man, if you don't let me use your phone... I'm gonna start singing Paula Abdul's greatest hits.
jokes.setup: Will trying to get phone access
jokes.punchline: Threatening to sing Paula Abdul as torture
jokes.explanation: Creative and unexpected threat. The specificity of Paula Abdul makes it funnier than generic bad singing threat.

jokes.text: Sir, give me that damn phone... or I'm gonna rip your liver out and eat it raw. / That's right, boy, and he about due for a snack.
jokes.setup: Phil demonstrates 'lawyer negotiation' after Will's Paula Abdul threat fails
jokes.punchline: Phil goes full violent threat, Will backs him up
jokes.explanation: Great character reversal - stuffy Phil goes street while Will supports. The 'due for a snack' addition is perfect.

jokes.text: Yes, sir, I'm a very, very bad butler. / In fact, I'm going to punish myself. / No phone privileges for a week.
jokes.setup: Phil yells at Geoffrey for letting Ashley drive
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically punishes himself by taking away his own phone privileges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey sarcasm and wit. The self-punishment is absurd and cleverly delivered.

jokes.text: In fact, I'm going to punish myself. No phone privileges for a week.
jokes.setup: Phil yelling at Geoffrey for being irresponsible
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's absurd self-punishment after hanging up
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Geoffrey moment - perfect deadpan absurdity. Self-imposed punishment after being yelled at is brilliant character consistency.

jokes.text: Not without any teeth, you won't.
jokes.setup: Hilary says she can still get a husband despite being superficial
jokes.punchline: Ashley threatens Hilary's teeth if she doesn't help
jokes.explanation: Good Ashley threat with perfect economy. Connects Hilary's vanity to the threat effectively.

jokes.text: That girl wanted me, Aunt Janice. / Carlton, when a woman says, 'Drop dead, troll...' it usually means she's not interested.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinks a girl at the salon was interested in him
jokes.punchline: She actually called him a troll and told him to drop dead
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton delusion setup with perfect brutal reality check. Great structure and character work.

jokes.text: I'm a kid. I don't know jack about insurance.
jokes.setup: Hospital clerk insists Ashley fill out insurance forms
jokes.punchline: Ashley states the obvious - she's too young to understand insurance
jokes.explanation: Good common sense moment from Ashley. Points out absurdity of bureaucracy.

jokes.text: Look, this is an emergency. Do you have any mascara?
jokes.setup: Hilary rushes into hospital for the 'emergency'
jokes.punchline: Her emergency is needing makeup, not the birth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - completely self-absorbed even during family emergency.

jokes.text: Wilma!
jokes.setup: Phil finally arrives to see his newborn
jokes.punchline: He calls Vivian the wrong name in the emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Phil's emotional confusion leads to wrong name. Moderately effective character moment.

jokes.text: Look, he look like a little black yoda.
jokes.setup: Everyone's looking at the newborn baby
jokes.punchline: Will compares the baby to Yoda from Star Wars
jokes.explanation: Classic Will inappropriate observation. The Star Wars reference works for the era and character.

jokes.text: That's a stupid name. / Why don't we name him after Daddy? Eli. / Great. / No, over my dead body. / Let's name him Rufus. / How about Brock?
jokes.setup: Family discussing what to name the baby
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous name suggestions that get worse
jokes.explanation: Standard escalating absurdity. Names get progressively worse but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Shazam!
jokes.setup: Continued name suggestions for the baby
jokes.punchline: Will suggests naming the baby after a superhero catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Appropriately silly Will suggestion. The superhero reference fits his character.

jokes.text: Wilma!
jokes.setup: Final family photo moment
jokes.punchline: Phil still hasn't learned his wife's name correctly
jokes.explanation: Running gag completion but not particularly strong. The repetition is the joke but it's worn thin.
